title: trifolium parryi subsp. montanense
intro: Trifolium parryi subsp. montanense, commonly known as mountain clover or mountain sweet clover, is a perennial plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Trifolium parryi subsp. montanense include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from coughs and colds. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
